elements.name: Title
texts: "We pray to ourself. The metal moon, unspent"

elements.name: Source
texts: Lough Derg', in Devlin, Dennis, Lough Derg and Other Poems, pp. 1-4, here p. 4

elements.name: Rights
texts: Citation for the purposes of criticism

elements.name: Text
texts:
“We pray to ourself. The metal moon, unspent
Virgin eternity sleeping in the mind,
Excites the form of prayer without content;
Whitehorn lightens, delicate and blind,
The negro mountain, and so, knelt on her sod,
This woman beside me murmuring My God! My God!”
